channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 142 replies — 341.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 142 • Updated: 2026-05-30 16:26 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283952
>>62271712
Bitcoin is a pyramid scheme and a waste of electricity. I was an early adopter of bitcoin fully thinking it was a scam and I sold mine in 2016 after I had enough to retire. I understand the whole point about fiat currency but what I think we need to do is just get rid of money all together and use assets as money like stocks and commodities. Money was invented so people didn't have to carry their assets around but know we all walk around with computers in our pocket that would allow us to trade our assets like how things used to be. I should be able to go to McDonalds and buy my food with corn for example. Bitcoin only exists for countries to bypass sanctions and if you buy bitcoin you are just giving more money to Putin and all the other scammers and terrorists.

62285517
>>62272225
They've got their preferences

62285578
>>62283375
Yeah, DeFi hacks are getting pretty crazy lately. That’s why multiple audits, formal verification, penetration testing, and cryptography reviews matter much more now with this much capital involved.

62285585
>>62272647
You'll regret your choices lol

62285589
>>62272667
You think you're smart?

62286335
>>62271712
No money, no job
They literally won't even take me at minimum wage places because I don't speak spanish.
So, no way to generate the income to buy BTC in the first place.

62287162
>>62274011
Work for it anon

62287177
>>62283952
You can already by McDonalds with corn. Use credit card and pay off credit card with corn futures. The problem is your dumbass is bleeding wealth by keeping your net worth in corn (or companies, or anything besides bitcoin) because people are going to make more corn (and ever faster each year due to tech advances) and inflate away your wealth.

62287383
>>62285578
but why remains the hack

62287392
>>62286335
If you're married at don't have money, you deserve a divorce

62287393
>>62271712
It's a gamble and I only bet on sure things. In fact I like to have my profits guaranteed under contract and insured BEFORE I spend a dime of my own money. I always sell BEFORE I buy so I know exactly how much I'm making in advance.

62287464
Most of the retards have been bled dry. BTC only exists now as a system for whales to lead exit pumps and bleed the occasional new entrants.

62287628
>>62287383
Most of the biggest DeFi disasters over the years involved bridges, custodians, or avoidable trust assumptions somewhere in the stack. That's exactly why security architecture gets attention now.

62287630
>>62280441
You're thinking the wrong way

62287632
>>62282426
Well, depends on them

62287634
BTC has just tracked the nasdaq for years until recently and now it just sucks ass no matter what happens.

Hype has peaked. Most of it is owned by boomers now and its not a hedge against anything it just dumps the hardest on bad news until boomers, banks and third worlders get tired of bagholding and it goes sub 20k forever

62287690
>>62287634
So you're saying btc is only a big hell hype?

62288099
>>62287634
nonsense

62288112
>>62273994
So it's not worth it?

62288281
>>62271712
Indifference. I got into the market when the BTC was $3K. but I bought Link instead. Pleased with my decision. I respect what it has done and continues to do with the world but I get excited for smart contracts more, which the BTC doesn't have.

62288653
>>62271712
Because I got caught with my pants down avgcost 95k, and I've been trimming it and using that money as play dump money. So far the 15k I sold from Btc and put into Sandisk have turned in 110k already, and the 10k into gold are at 14k, and it's an actual asset. Never buying that retarded shit again. I still have like 30k and maybe I'll keep that, but chances are I'll probably sell and ride the SpaceX pump and dumo with it. Put in 50k into it, sell 50k when it doubles in price, keep the rest as gains. Did the same shit with Micron, Sandisk and silver, take profits and just keep the rest. This is a hype based market and btc has no hype anymore.

62290061
>>62288112
We'll find out when TBVs actually launch.
Then you can judge the product based on what it does, not on what random people online tell you to think.

62290076
>>62275451
Ok, just buy altcoins

62290078
>>62272002
ahahahahahahahah

62290085
>>62275501
If BTC goes to zero, alts are probably even more cooked.

62290480
>>62287464
If it's such an obvious scam, why are you still here watching it?

62290856
>>62271933
There's truth to this, but I dunno about 40k. Buy "dips" now I say

62290931
>>62272803
>>62272874

The conundrum that niggers on here don't understand is that everyone wants upside volatility, but it's the volatility itself that ultimately creates the ceiling.

It's the cute naive chick that ceases to be so once you fuck her. You can't have your cute and fuck it too.

62291823
>>62290931
You're also another nigger

62292614
>>62287393
So how much are you making?

item.title: The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies — 51 replies — 204.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies

Open thread • Replies: 51 • Updated: 2026-05-30 16:50 UTC

OP

BAT bros? You still there? What the fuck is going on? LINK shits. BTC dumps deadly diarrhea. Orange man shitposts. But this piece of shit is +15%? Is Lobes flipping the switch? Hold me. Also. Jennie lewds please

Early replies (first 3)

62285628
no one still owns BAT
... right?

62285632
>>62285628
Apparently I do. Jennie doesn't though

62285713
YOU KNOW THE DEAL.

TP - WHERE ARE MY THOTS AT? IT'S BEEN A WHILE.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62289108
>>62286063
>>62286122
jolly good jiggling jugs TP
>>62286450
seconding moar of this btggf or even her username

62289174
How is this board still discussing dead shitcoins from 10 years ago
Does no one here like making money

62289182
>>62287337
lmao she do be living high off the BAT scam

62289202
>>62289174
BAT threads are just places for us to lament and to talk to the friends we met along the way

62289359
>>62286122
what the fuck is this whore's name

62289403
>>62286122
>gnarly memory leaks
no its working damn fine on my machine
I had to fix youtube by element blocking the share links though

62289404
>>62286122
Man

62289650
>>62287337
The fuck you doing? We were supposed to keep this from the peasants

62290320
>>62287337
you mean her name isn't even jennie? wtf scammed

62290344
>>62289108
Hey Lad! Long time, no see. I'll switch PCs in a bit and see if I can find the profile for you.

>>62289359
Give me a bit and I'll see what I can do for you. I have like 8 more from that set and some other ones in the meantime I can post.

>>62289403
I'm getting like 5GB+ memory usage from a single YouTube tab. Tends to only happen on livestreams or really long videos. Let me know if you have a request!

>>62290122
Are you requesting feet?

62290355
>>62287337
That's so foul, blowing up her page like that. She better private that shit ASAP.

>>62287362
I'm working on it, but here's another thicc goth for (You).

62290540
>>62290344
>I'm getting like 5GB+ memory usage from a single YouTube tab]
its a known bug yt's end, the like and share (etc) interface below the video is caught in an endless live loop
to fix it do this: go to settings then find shields then select content filtering then scroll down to create custom filters and paste this in the box field www.youtube.com###menu
then save changes
let me know how you get on

62290561
>>62290344
>I'll switch PCs in a bit and see if I can find the profile for you.
thanks TP, you're the best of 'em
>Hey Lad! Long time, no see
I'm working abroad again
trying to get settled in
what about you? doing well I hope

62290662
>>62290540
I really appreciate that, lad! Thanks a lot! I'll give it a go in a bit.

>>62290561
>you're the best of 'em
Back at you!
>I'm working abroad again
Where do they have you this time?

62290702
>>62290662
yw TP, that fix is a life saver, but if you're still having issues then tell me as I have a few more filters that will help
back in kong of the hong variety for as long as needed
hot damn that is one holy soft thicc and wobbly thot

62290728
>>62290344
this bitch is just ridiculous

62290732
>>62290662
HOLY FUCK LORD HAVE MERCY

62290812
>>62290702
Seems to be doing a lot better. I really appreciate it!
>HK
sick! I might be going to Shanghai sometime later in the Summer. If I make it out there, I'll let you know. Hope you have a comfy stay!

>>62290728
>>62290732
I got the name for you >>62286122
Cece Rose. I'll dump more of the OF if you don't want to pay for it.

_
Might get a vacay for this one, but fuck it.

62290849
>>62290812
cool TP
yeah let me know
this apartment is way better than the last place I was assigned thank fuck
kekeke we'll see you next thread ig but worth it lel
jannies might have chilled tho I hope

62290910
>>62290812
LEGEND!

I'm gonna need the name of this goth whore next. Are those braces?

62291155
>>62290355
>>62290662
>>62290812
Fucking hell, TP. More of this please, especially >>62290355

62291188
>>62285628
Nope. Sold everything when it became clear they’re not actually interested in building utility for the token. It’s a memecoin connected to a browser. They had the world by the balls and they made a conscious decision to do absolutely nothing for BAT. They cannot be trusted

62291222
>>62290355
You're a pornographer, the fuck you care?

62291254
>>62286450
>>62290355
>>62290662
>>62290812
TP MOGS /gif/
THE MOTHERFUCKING GOAT

62291656
>>62290812
Wonderful rack on her. Lets see some asian MILFS GILFS

62291660
>>62287337
she's hands down the hottest crypto girl, the hottest thing about her is that she hides how big her ass is, won't even show it off out of modesty

62291667
>>62289174
Most BAT holders moved on to something else eventually, but still hold some BAT out of loyalty and the purest copium you'll ever find

Anyone who was "all-in" on BAT has sold or killed themselves by now

62292238
>>62286450
need more of this one lads

62292673
>>62291660
i want to see her tiddies so bad

item.title: Honestly owning rentals made me realize how helpless alot of adults are now. — 49 replies — 196.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Honestly owning rentals made me realize how helpless alot of adults are now.

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-05-30 16:37 UTC

OP

Honestly owning rentals made me realize how helpless alot of adults are now.

I bought a bunch of duplexes down south back around 2018-2020 because the numbers made sense. Cheap property taxes, cheap insurance, cheap properties in general. One duplex I paid $124k for is bringing in around $1925/mo total now and the tennants renewed instantly after the last increase.

Had a tennant last week who submitted a maintenence request because the microwave “stopped heating.” Property manager drove over there and the door wasnt shut all the way.

Another guy called because his screen door came off the track. PM slid it back in place in about 10 seconds and the guy goes “much obliged sir” like he just repaired farm equipment from the 1800s or something.

Whats weird is the older tennants almost never bother anybody. Pay on time. Maybe ask for a sink fix every few years. Younger ones send paragraphs over tiny stuff.

Not even trying to sound mean but I geniunely think people are less capable now than they were 20 years ago.

People online act like owning rentals is some impossible genius level thing but honestly half the job is just dealing with grown adults who dont know why the wifi stopped working after they unplugged the router to move furniture around.

Early replies (first 3)

62283853
>>62283816
It never sounds worth the money

62283860
How much of your revenue do you devote to hookers

62283871
>>62283816
I will buy one of these when I make it from Link. Probably in a college town. I won’t care about any profits but negotiate BJ’s for rent if they are unable to pay

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62291886
>>62283816
>young people are less experience than older people
Wow, I mean, really? Jesus, this needs to be studied. Oh my god... It's crazy to even think about. Groundbreaking

62291888
>>62283816
Nigger I am not fixing my own fucking shit that is YOUR JOB FAGGOT NOW COME REPLACE MY FRIDGE WHILE I PUT HOLES IN YOUR WALLS
I AM NOT FIXING THEM OR CLEANING WHEN I LEAVE

62291926
>>62286915
Part of the basis of taking half of someone’s income for owning an asset is that you give a shit about at least maintaining the value, in theory

62291931
>>62283816
>appliances
is it normal to have these in the property? Dishwasher, hot water system, aircons yeah, but all the other appliances the tenants usually bring themselves in my country.

62291934
>>62283816
had a tenant keep complaining that the dishwasher wasn't working. maintenance bloke went around and had a look. the tenant wasn't scraping uneaten food off the plates, including bones, and blocking up the dishwasher.

62291936
>>62283816
Once had a tenant say I needed to organise a sparky because the bathroom down lights weren't working. Took him a long time to realise he needed to change the light bulbs.

62291959
>>62283816
nigger you ask for a lot of money you better fix EVERYTHING

62291962
>>62284369
cool. better fix it if you want money for it, retard

62291978
>>62283816
cant think of a worse investment desu.

ai/robot wave incoming.
even if they don't replace everyone, commies are right that it is a big stick capital can use to beat down wages.

if you're a landlord, that's all you're doing, extracting wages.
if they're fucked so are you.
people with money to burn aren't renting for $1600 a month.

on top of that the laws get worse every year even don is pushing rules to make more competitive cheap housing available, so you're gunna get fucked from both sides of the isle whether its by rules or more competition.

if you're a hassid in brooklyn who's inheriting the property their grandpappy bought in 1930 and who has a network of fellas in a similar line with established political connections as part of a lobby group going back 50 years with deals for protection in place, that could make sense. same if you're some big company.

but being some lone owner from out of state paying x2 covid prices when a bunch of people are going to be put out of work and when the government (both R & D) is expanding housing/increasing legislation seem like a recipe for getting raped.

62291980
>>62283816
selection bias

competent adults are NOT renting from you

62291998
How old are you? You didn't mention paying off loans for the houses just maintenance expenses

62292024
>>62283816
Europoor here, holy shit what a mentally fucked economic scheme that is. At first I couldnt believe it running your numbers (18% interest), then it made sense knowing what your customers are (bluark peepul). Still fucked up. This sounds so extremely jewish.

62292036
Yes, tenants are retarded animals. I dropped that biz due to it.

62292070
>>62284771
>it was cheap
That’s why

62292199
>>62292024
What the absolute fuck are you going on about?

62292220
>>62291978
maybe. but it's an asset that is always needed. it will always have value and you'll always be able to rent it, except of course absolute shit happens like it burning down or being squatted.

>>62283816
When i took my appartment, the previous renter had : door not closing properly (main door)and couldnt be fully locked, induction plate not working, a leak in the bathroom, cold air going under the window, many other stuff. I don't know how she lived so long like that. i fixed everything myself for like 300$, even the holes under the windows wood.

renters don't care or don't dare to do anything because they feel like they could end up in some trouble. when i fixed the door i used some power tools to recalibrate it and i took the chance i could absolutely fail. also changed the locks which were 30 years old didn't want any retard to have keys to my appt

62292222
>>62291931
sometimes a microwave will be built in to the kitchen cabinetry. otherwise a microwave is something the tenant would bring. the one exception I can think of is short term rentals where it’s unrealistic for the tenant to bring one.

62292243
>>62283964
You know there are local people that are more talented than "globofix" right??
Right?

62292248
>>62292222
ANYONE that uses a microwave is a fucking total retard.
Same with a dishwasher.
>JUST PAY HUNDREDS OF DOLLARS TO POISON YOURSELF AND RUN UP YOUR ELECTRIC BILL
>everyone signs up

I'm rich by the standards of 99% of people. Own home, land, cars, business, no debt, $500,000 liquid, PPE at business in millions.
I don't have a microwave and I removed the dishwasher to put a little wine rack.
More kitchen space. Dishes just get done after you use them and then they dry on the rack and you use them again without ever putting them away.
PACK A LUNCH AND A GOOD ONE.

62292317
>>62283816
I truly have zero desire to be a landlord but I do have an odd curiosity.
I have to wonder...how would one slumlord-max? Like, how do you take advantage of section 8 kind of housing and not give a shit about fuck while still collecting money from the government through the niggas? You know they're gonna level the place so you don't give a fuck about property value...so then...where's the breakeven? How do you profit of that kind of shit?

62292327
I hate the concept of landing and dealing w/ ubermench but I want to buy houses for my kids so they can inherit that cheap tax rate and God knows what a house will cost in 30 years

62292339
>>62292317
>how would one slumlord-max?
buy a shitty run down building and rent it out to poor people with wrecked credit but a steady job (i.e., don't rent it to jobless niggers who will never pay a dime and turn it into an EPA super fund site with crack cocaine residue.) these people have so few options that they won't dare complain to local government, because it means they'll be homeless.

you can't do this with section 8 because someone from the federal government will occasionally inspect the apartment for habitability etc.

62292353
>>62283816
>Not even trying to sound mean but I geniunely think people are less capable now than they were 20 years ago.
learned helplessness

62292360
>>62292339
That almost feels kind of philanthropic, in a weird way. I mean, if they're broke niggas but have steady jobs, there is a chance they'll actually better themselves. And if you can profit off of it in the meantime, why not?

62292458
>>62292360
just take the extra step and make the place semi decent to live anon. don't be a sicko like that

62292479
Owning rentals is a horrible investment.
Famously Trump underperformed s&p 500 by billions. he would be better off financially selling his inheritance, putting it all in s&p 500 and watching anime all day.

And that was before housing became extremely overpriced. It literally can't go up 10x in inflation adjusted terms, it would put an average person out on the street. It can go up nominally with inflation, but same is true for the stock market.

Just sell this crap and buy s&p 500

there are other arguments for owning a rental - like buy a house in a different country so you have somewhere to run away to if things get really bad. but it's not what the average person is doing.

62292484
>>62292360
it depends on how jewish you want to be. i've done walkthroughs on slumlord homes. some of them have rotting wood, collapsed sub-floors, and black mold in the bathrooms, non-functional electric baseboard heat that would probably burn the place down if you used it, and so on. i saw a 3 story multifamily built in the early 1900s with a stone foundation that was crumbling. the basement was a jungle gym of screw jacks keeping the place from collapsing. several families were actively living in the building.

62292596
>>62292479
Its good to have multiple income streams and real estate often offers significant tax advantages not to mention its much easier to secure low interest loans. Of course like any other investment you need to look for low prices but high value, even today there are many markets across the US where there is good value to be had.

62292607
>>62292596
you can easily get a securities backed loan too, at low collateral rates it will be cheaper than a mortgage.
conversely, stocks are only so much better because average person defaults to real estate

62292634
>>62283816
The funny thing about landlord stories like this is that they're built around a kind of selection bias.

You don't remember the hundreds of tenants who quietly handle small problems themselves. You remember the one who submitted a maintenance ticket because the microwave door wasn't fully closed. That story sticks because it's unusual, not because it's representative.

And honestly, people tend to overestimate how much a single anecdote proves. Someone making a mistake with an appliance doesn't mean adults have suddenly become helpless. It means a person encountered something that appeared broken and reported it through the channel available to them.

The same logic could be applied anywhere. A customer asks an employee where something is instead of searching the entire store. A homeowner calls a mechanic for a noise that turns out to be minor. A business owner hires an accountant because they don't understand tax law. Most people rely on specialists for things outside their expertise. That's normal, not evidence of decline.

What's interesting is that the economics of the story tell a different tale than the conclusion. A duplex purchased for $124k generating roughly $1,925 a month in rent suggests the tenants are providing a significant return on the asset. They're not passive observers in that arrangement; they're the reason the investment performs in the first place.

So if the strongest example of widespread helplessness is someone misdiagnosing a microwave, that's actually a pretty weak case. Human beings have always overlooked obvious things. We notice those moments because they're memorable, not because they're new.

item.title: how do you avoid your infos being leaked ans the subsequent home jacking? — 44 replies — 176.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
how do you avoid your infos being leaked ans the subsequent home jacking?

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-05-30 16:39 UTC

OP

France has seen an explosion in crypto kidnapping. This explosion came after the state demanded citizens to declare their "self hosted" wallets and their balances. There also was a data leak from some kind of private investment company after a hack.
People with balances as low as 2 btc have been targetted and successfully forced to give up all their assets under threats. Some people living abroad have had their relatives kidnapped.

How do you avoid this shit? all the regulations on crypto have been an absolute disaster, the only end goal is to steal your wealth via taxation and state control. This totally ruins the whole crypto thing. There is no point in holding large amounts of cryptos if it puts a target on your back and on your familly and you can't sleep well at night.
Now many anons might think this is a french thing but eventually more leaks will happen and nobody will be safe. there will be lists with your name and address and all the purchase you did in the last years, even maybe your depo address that criminals will be able to track and see what most likely happened with your balance.

One simple mitigation measure would be to use many exchanges, many adresses, as to make it appear you hold less, but this is also an annoying process that require strict management and doesn't guarantee additional leaks at the state level if they group all your trading infos.
You should stop using any KYC, stop paying taxes on government that don't care at all about your safety, and go full down the crypto ecosystem giving up off ramps as much as you can.

Early replies (first 3)

62287742
Generic India hate thread disguised as something else.

62287753
>>62287731
>France
That's what you get for living in Africa.

62287759
>>62287753
while i can't disagree, the problem remains. if you think living in the usa or germany makes you safe from this issue you're delulu. Usa is known for its violent crime just wait til a list of easy 1 Mil++ robberies on individual citizens start going around and see what happens

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287985
>>62287731
1. lie
2. do not ever disclose your balance
3. have 2 homes in case

62288016
>>62287931
very forward thinking anon.
Difference is with a bank account it's much more anonymous and you can't just get robbed in a wrench key attack.

62288126
>>62287892
>KYC exchange
retard. THE FUCKING STATE IS MAKING YOU DO IT

62288135
>>62287985
>GOY ID
Kek has spoken.

62288136
>>62287759
>easy robberies
Nigga I will kill anyone who comes around trying to rob me. I am armed and insane.

62288150
>>62288136
Yeah, imagine giving up your seed phrase to some angry African because it has a gun that it barely knows how to use.

62288560
>>62287731
that video brought me so much joy, thank you.

62289395
>>62288560
i couldnt imagine a stupider gimmick than a rollerblade setup in a high-end, indoor restaurant, where alot of soup, stew and curry-style foodstuffs are served.

It makes sense in an outdoors setting like in america, where you're serving lightweight packaged food, and its probably more efficient overall to use rollerblades. But this is pajeet-tarded.

62289432
>>62289395
Rollerblade service should only be done by red headed tomboys with short hair and freckles with a small chest, a bubble butt, and wide hips.

62289484
>>62288150
>>62288136
how about they kidnap your elderly parents or any other important relative and start cutting their fingers live if you don't give the seed? which is their new modus operandi and they did it in france to a guy who is living in dubai but his father lives here

62289530
>>62287731
1. Dont live in france
1. Dont live in europe
2. Have multiple dummy wallet and load it with small amounts of stables and some shitcoins
3. Dont live in france

62289586
>>62289432
Too bad here is your skinny gay jeet. Fuck you.

t. Jew that doesn’t deal in the real world

62289590
>>62287742
do not capitalize the word "india"

62289690
>>62289586
>t. Jew
we smell it. choke on a baby dick.

62289723
>>62287731
Lmao.

62289923
>>62289432
>>62289590
based takes

>>62289484
sounds like in that particular case, the guy was realllllly retarded and publicly shitposted his wealth on social media. If he lives in dubai, he doesn't need to declare shit as income retard.

62289932
>>62289923
he was just running a crypto related business in dubai. I'm telling you it's a matter of time before this happens more or less everywhere where death penatly isn't the norm for this shit

62289939
>>62289923
you think it's still a matter of bragging, we are well past that point. Criminals gangs have lists of people and their assets either from hacks, or government officials selling those infos. they hire street level retards thugs to do the job from another country. Somehow the criminals found out he was the ceo of some business in dubai, probably not very hard to find.

62289944
>>62289932
so then it had nothing to do with Franceistan's laws, and it was just a case of an exposed person getting exploited.

Police need to do alot more to curb organised crime, but Anarcho-tyranny is the order of the day in the west, commies are just plain evil.

62290407
>>62287731
>own firearms
>shoot home invaders
gee that wasn't so hard now was it?

62291194
>>62287731
>declare their "self hosted" wallets and their balances

There's the problem. Never declare shit and go through monero or other privacy coins to protect your privacy.

62291200
>>62287731
this doesn't happen where I live because we often execute criminals while they're engaged in the crime.

62291231
>>62287731
If you live as a rich man in a poor society, you are a target.
If you live as middle class in a middle class society, your community can protect you.

This was discovered during Americans golden age, and has now been forgotten.
I look forward to the horrors of war visiting the small minded selfish cunts who currently run the world.

62291723
>>62287731
wrench attacks only work if you fear death. can't find my crypto if i'm dead fags

62292465
>>62287731
>France has seen an explosion in crypto kidnapping. This explosion came after the state demanded citizens to declare their "self hosted" wallets and their balances. There also was a data leak from some kind of private investment company after a hack.
People with balances as low as 2 btc have been targetted and successfully forced to give up all their assets under threats. Some people living abroad have had their relatives kidnapped.
This data is incorrect. First, france didn't pass the law that demands the citizens to declare their self hosted wallet. France planned to pass this law, but the recent increase in crypto related kidnappings made France reconsider passing this law.
About the fiscal data leak in france, it was a leak from the fiscal software "Mira", which contains data from french tax payers. A public servant in the tax administration named (false name) "Ghalia C.", consulted information from the "Mira" database, without authorization, and sold this tax information (assets, physical adress) to criminal networks, who then used this information to locate and attack people with crypto.

62292476
>>62292465
the declarations has been retracted, but it was passed and many fools did declare.
And people near me, in a middle class area next to a big city have been victim of home jacking with a balance less than 2btc.
Not exactly sure where you're trying to go there?

62292491
>>62287731
The only real solution is multisigs in different bank vaults. It creates friction between you and the funds. And it put the defense of the funds on the bank.

62292604
>>62289432
Holy based, unfortunately this doesn't exist

62292639
>>62292476
>Not exactly sure where you're trying to go there?
The recent attacks in france are still a continuity, caused, by the Ghalia C. leak.
That is the database the criminals networks are still taking their informations from.

So the problem is not the declaration obligation per se, but the fact that the french fiscal administration is currently not competent to manage sensitive information.
Obviously the declaration obligation put more risk on the crypto holders, especially if the fiscal data is not secured in france, others leaks could happen.
This would probably have not happened in america, as their sensitive digital data is actually secured. And if a leak happen, you can be sure that they will find the person responsible.

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 64 replies — 72.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 64 • Updated: 2026-05-30 15:58 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285770
>>62285733
Gonna look into it
tks anon

62285791
>>62285480
The moon creates the tides

62285803
>>62285791
>wild shrimp need tides to reproduce
>moon creates tides
>moon makes shrimp sandwiches possible
Thanks moon

62285830
>>62285103
>https://shiyu-coder.github.io/Kronos-demo/
this shit looks overfit nigga id backtest it at least

62285839
I have a similar model coded up in pine it is quite useless lol

62285878
>>62285839
lmao this one works a little better, my main problem was underestimating the violence we got on fairly faint signals this last 3 days, which might be partially the nature of the simulation - outputs start to get a little blurry 4-5 weeks out and then just turn into noise, which is why I do the updates every 4 weeks
Still, calling every major pivot this month using schizo math is pretty decent

62285897
>>62285878
shut up bot

62285919
>>62285897
no u

62285940
>>62285919
you probably work for the cia retard

62285967
>>62285940
>CIA
>giving away the free money cheat code to internet autists
doesn't seem very likely anon

62286025
>>62285967
I mean as long as this doesn't go full premium (as a ploy to ensnare gullible /biz/ tards) it seems like a good resource. Some ppl on this board want to stay poor or just FUD for fun. Pathetic behavior honestly.

62286076
>>62286025
It's nowhere close to where I'd feel comfortable charging people money for it. Trade bots/API feeds will probably be subscription, when that's done, but those are more for pro traders
There's also daily updates on the twitter account

62286347
>>62285878
>outputs start to get a little blurry 4-5 weeks out and then just turn into noise, which is why I do the updates every 4 weeks
Why not update weekly? or even daily?

62286447
>>62286347
>Why not update weekly? or even daily?
Basically, because it's a lot easier - there are a lot of inputs into the model that each have to be harvested and processed by me before they get fed into the sim. It runs into the hours each time. I can also cope that the current date's "conditions" are obviously the starting point of the forecast, so having them flattening out a little bit is easier to align
I do weekly updates on the site and the twitter account, but that's 90% just applying current price momentum and FX momentum to the existing physics-based forecast, which I have to get better at. I'm a nerd, not a trader

62286532
>>62284814
>buy may 17
>sell for a loss on May 25

thanks

62286552
>>62286532
>buy may 17
>short may 25
you're welcome

62286639
>>62286447
>because it's a lot easier
Fair enough. Although I'm sure at least some of that data gathering and processing could be automated.
>I'm a nerd, not a trader
So you don't use it all? I get that some people are very risk averse but if you're confident in your technology I don't see why you wouldn't at least dip your toes and make what is essentially free money.

I think I might do a bit of paper trading and I'll let you know how it pans out by the end of June.

62286700
>>62286639
>Fair enough. Although I'm sure at least some of that data gathering and processing could be automated.
A lot of it is! I keep getting tempted to outsource some of it to an LLM but some gut instinct says "you'd be really really dumb to do that," even a local one
>So you don't use it all? I get that some people are very risk averse but if you're confident in your technology I don't see why you wouldn't at least dip your toes and make what is essentially free money.
Oh, I use it, have been trading perps with it for years, getting more profitable as the model evolves. After the addition of the FX layer last spring, I can get away with some truly disgusting leverage. I just meant I don't have a background in trading or technical analysis, so I've been learning all of those skills on the go. It's all related but still a different skillset
>I think I might do a bit of paper trading and I'll let you know how it pans out by the end of June.
Please do, I'll post in here when there's updates but you can always get in touch via Twitter

62287275
>>62284814
I’m just gonna wait for the mega dump to flush the longs down to 60k..

62289468
>>62284814
L, and let me be perfectly clear, FG

62289671
>>62289468
So up from here? Don’t speak zoomzoom

62289838
>>62289671
This is where price should turn up - hybrid FX+physics says Buy because FX is so positive, physics officially flips green tomorrow, it all seems pretty good for bulls over the next 24 hours.

62289954
>>62287275
this. I just go all in bottom of the bear market and do nothing a couple years

62291704
>>62284814
This is no proper backtesting! You must test at least 30 instances!
https://youtu.be/zXoZdnYfIlA?si=MPB49se3bA1H2fnp

62291711
>>62291704
*32 Instances

62291719
>>62291711
>>62291704
?

62291779
>>62291719
How big are the wins and how big are the losses? They have to be in a profitable proportion together with the win rate. 66% win rate with wins 1/3 as big as the losses is not profitable for example.

62291951
>>62284814
broken clock o algo

62292012
what time of day do you make the trades

62292563
if you have a profitable strategy then just make a hyperliquid vault so we can join it and you have 10% profit share

item.title: SATA bought over 2k BTC this week — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
SATA bought over 2k BTC this week

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-05-30 16:39 UTC

OP

The new investment offering 13% in daily dividends bought 2k BTC this week.

Strive the company behind SATA only owns 15k BTC so far so this is huge.

It's marked to have another huge day today probably going to finish around another 500 BTC bought.

Early replies (first 3)

62288571
wow 2K btc

to the moon

62288604
>>62288571
They grew their BTC treasury by 10% in one week.
2k BTC is more than El Salvador bought in the last 2 years.

This is rocket fuel because everyone is getting 13% and BTC is getting slurped up.
This will send the price to 250k in a year.

62290655
Holy Bitcoin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62291854
>>62288531
>13% APR paid daily
We understand that this is unsustainable, right? This is an obscene amount to be paying out, it cannot last forever at this percentage. What's their exit strategy? Is there any?

62292377
>>62291854
It's 13% for the year, if you auto buy more with the dividend it's 13.88% the dividend pays everyday.

They're strategy is that Bitcoin goes up 30%+ a year and they make loads of money.

They're chief risk officer interviewed coffeezilla and destroyed him in a debate, it's legit.

62292430
>>62290655
So they literally just copied Saylors' Ponzi scheme but threw an extra 3% payout on top to make sure it collapses faster.

62292449
>>62288531
Oh boy oh boy, if these schemes don't collapse in the next 2 years, it might literally the catalyst of next bear market bottom.

Saylor's migrated some bitcoin to Coinbase and Nakamoto Inc. selling and reporting huge losses.

I hope these niggers in fancy suits are taking into account that btc will at highest reach 200k next bull

https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/crypto/articles/bitcoin-firm-nakamotos-stock-hits-150928904.html

62292487
>>62292377
I like Jeff Walton. I like Michael Saylor. I don't own mstr or asst or any of the preferreds. I want to see how the market reacts when a bitcoin bull market kicks off. I know they'll be able to issue the common stock as mnav goes up. My curiosity is what happens to the preferred shares? I know it's a variable rate and it will inevitably force a raise to keep it at par but will that be enough or will they have to buyback the shares as well? So now instead of using funds to increase the bitcoin exposure, they're forced to deleverage. Then to make the common stock attractive again, they either issue debt to lever up the balance sheet or sell bitcoin to buyback the common stock. It's not a ponzi but it's extremely cyclical unless bitcoin does just go up only but it still seems extremely cyclical as well. Definitely interesting but I'd rather just hold bitcoin.

62292499
>>62292449
if they can remain solvent until 2029 they won

62292640
>>62288531
Fuck that theyre run by that poojeet vivek ramaswamy. All bizraelies should buy microstrategy and support the white man. Not to mention poojeets history of scamming people with his old biotech company

62286993

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287042
>>62286974

62287046
>>62287007
Either this or emergency unpaid overtime shifts.

62287049
KWAB

62287052
CLEAN UP THE DEBRIS, WAGIE!!!

62287125
>>62286974
bezos can suck the shit of my asshole

62287128
>>62286974
So? Eill stonks go down and crypto up now? No? So?

62287260
>>62286974
AMAZON x XRP PARTNERSHIP?????????

BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62287271
>>62287052
what an ugly freak god damn nigga even with 270 billion dollars being this ugly has got to be rough.

62287278
Gender studies be one of them classes where you realize society really out here making random rules then acting like them rules dropped from the sky . The whole subject talks about how people get treated different based on gender, race, class, and identity. It ain’t just about “men versus women.” Nah, it’s deeper than that. It’s about power, who got it, and who keeps the system running like a broken carnival ride .

Back in the day, society pushed these roles heavy. Dudes supposed to be tough, work jobs, never cry. Girls supposed to stay pretty, quiet, cook food, and smile through suffering like customer service employees on minimum wage . Gender studies basically walks into the room and says, “Yo, who invented these goofy rules?” That’s where people start talking about gender as a social construct. Meaning society made a lot of these expectations up over time and everybody just downloaded the update without reading the terms and conditions .

Another big thing in gender studies is intersectionality. Big word, simple meaning. Different struggles stack together like bad Wi-Fi connections. Somebody can face problems because of gender, race, money, or all three at once. The system don’t hit everybody equally. Some people start life on easy mode while others spawn into expert difficulty with no tutorial .

Media also catches strays in this class because movies, ads, music, and social media keep pushing stereotypes 24/7. Folks grow up seeing the same roles repeated until they think it’s “normal.” Gender studies tries to challenge those dominant narratives and make people question why society moves the way it does.

At the end of the day, the class teaches that identity, power, and culture all connected. Also teaches students how capitalism be profiting off stereotypes while everybody else just trying to survive and pay rent .

62287284
NO BATHROOM BREAKS TOMORROW

12 HOUR SHIFTS SAT AND SUN

62287347
>>62287260
so bullihs at this..

62287354
>>62287271
He literally looks like any other MF in the street

62287575
>>62286974

>didn't even leave the ground

62287805

62290349
>>62286974

>>62290349

62290608
>>62286974

62290641
>>62286979

62290658
>>62290349

Precisely. Zero niggers. The white man will have finally have peace.

62290712
>>62290658

62292472

62292498

item.title: Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. — 61 replies — 19.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now.

Open thread • Replies: 61 • Updated: 2026-05-30 13:40 UTC

OP

Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. AMA law related /biz/ questions you may have.

Early replies (first 3)

62272819
>>62272812
Can i peepee in the poopoo or will i go to jail?

62272833
>>62272819
You may

62272835
>>62272812
Is it legal for a president to collab with a news outlet to pump & dump the stock market while insiders make bank on it?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62277022
>>62275816
>170 LSAT

Acceptable.

>Mid 20s Law School

Acceptable.

>3.5 1L GPA

Acceptable.

>I’m RAing with a professor and doing a one month internship with a local judge later in the summer.

It’s over. You are cooked. BIG oof. Thanks for playing.

62277039
>>62277022
Why is it over? And why didn’t you address my penis size?

62277080
>>62277039
If you are not interning at an AMLAW 50 firm your 1L summer then you literally need to kill yourself.

[spoiler]My posts are intended to satirize the rampant one-upsmanship in the legal profession. I don’t actually care about your genitalia you dumb retard. It is a metaphor.[/spoiler]

62278084
>>62277080
Well don’t scare me like that anon. You freaked me out since some of the people in my class do have Big Law internships and it’s kinda tough not to feel jealous sometimes with how much they’re getting paid.

62278202
>>62278084
Funposting aside, (You) should be (mildly) freaked out because spending your 1L summer split between volunteering for a professor and a one (1) month stint with a (non-federal) jjudge is concerning. Especially if the stats (You) quoted me earlier are true.

(You) are underachieving.

62278255
>>62278202
I don't know what to tell you. I applied for a billion 1L summer positions and even got a lot of interviews but none of them panned out. It would have been nice to get something more prestigious looking but I'm just glad I finally got something lined up. i have heard also that as long as you do do something legal related 1L summer then firms don't really care what it was, is that not true?

I'm not actually aiming for Big Law or anything, I'm mostly interested in environmental and real estate law and potentially being a prosecutor. I dunno I've only taken the basic classes and it feels weird this expectation to know for certain what you want to do before you've learned anything about the field.

62278315
>>62278255
I appreciate your sincerity, and I will stop frogposting.

Forget about prestige. Focus on what will get you paid. I encourage you to network with local boutique and midsize firms. These days, Big Law internships are reserved for black lesbians (unironically). You’re doing fine.

Godspeed.

62278366
>>62278315
>Forget about prestige. Focus on what will get you paid
I am getting paid for the RA job though.
>I encourage you to network with local boutique and midsize firms
This might be self-evident as a 4chan poster but I absolutely hate networking, really suck at it, and would like to avoid it as much as possible. Is it really that essential to getting a good job? Do I just have to force myself to do it? I heard the 2L summer hiring process was supposed to be way chiller and less insane.

And thanks for your advice by the way!

62278395
>>62278366
Networking is somewhat required for any career path unfortunately. My condolences anon

62280144
>>62278395

62280365
>>62278366
You will miss out of some of the best opportunities in this profession if you don’t network, unfortunately. I only got my current job because one of my professors was willing to refer me to someone on the inside.

62280758
Are there any good legal loopholes for evading debt collection? I'm writing a story, and there's a chapter where the MC scrambles to hide his assets from the chapter 7 trustee.

62281712
>>62280758
I know several but will tell you none

62282639
>>62280758
>I'm writing a story
I dunno if I believe you there anon

62286326
>>62272812
Truebit was hacked in January you think a lawsuit has a chance?

62286469
>>62272812
How much are jews over-represented in law?

Can I get in with a science degree and mediocre GPA (3.5)? If i can't get a science job with my MS then thinking of pivoting into patent law.

62286482
what are the reputations of people who go into different types of law? like are torts people regarded as ambulance chasers? people who go into real estate considered retards? etc

62286539
how do I sue companies for discrimination during the interview process?

62286591
>>62273041
>the irish people have done nothing for humanity, and neither will you. You are niggers compared to Russians

62286708
>>62273090
What were your classmates like? Like how nursing is single moms and programming is white sexual deviants and engineering students are just a bunch of Aryan chads.

62286727
>>62272812
Kys lawfag

62286755
>>62286326
Probably not
>>62286469
Definitely overrepresented but I dunno how much.
>>62286482
I definitely know personal injury lawyers are seen as being kind of skeavy, the civil rights and public interest stereotype is they're very wealthy and privileged with their parents paying for everything but still act condescending towards everyone trying to get paid. I don't really know stereotypes for the rest.
>>62286539
You basically can't unless you have a smoking gun like emails between each other saying they were discriminating or they accidentally sent you a voicemail joking about that guy they just discriminated against
>>62286708
Probably 60% women, and 95% white other than one asian girl. There were a few different cliques but the only really shared trait was alcoholism and being nerds. Some are the friendly type of nerds and some the smug type of nerds. Like there was one guy who would go to TA office hours and when someone would ask the TA a question he would very quickly butt in and try to answer the question for the TA, then at the end ask the TA "is that basically right?" pissed people off. Most people were just normal though.
>>62286727
Why come?

62287528
I know you said it's only for /biz/ questions, but I'm really curious: do you think Jigsaw could ever be prosecuted? This is something we're regularly discussing on >>>/tv/.

62287539
>>62272812
Is gooning illegal if she doesn't notice?

62289826
>>62287528
The guy who kidnaps people and puts them in death traps? What would even be the argument for him not being guilty of murder? To be guilty of murder and found to be the cause of someone’s death, you have to be the but-for cause of their death (but for the defendant doing X, person would not have died) and the proximate cause (basically means them dying was foreseeable from defendants actions). But-for Jigsaw putting them in the traps, they would not have died and their deaths were extremely foreseeable
>>62287539
Yes it’s illegal, but you can’t be charged with anything you’re the only one who knows about

62290013
>>62287539
bit of gooning on the side dont hurt anybody

62290634
how pretigious do you place your job in society? is there any job you think is more prestigious?

62290695
oh and by "prestigious" i mean being respected and earning big bucks, not like firefighter-prestige

62292185
>>62290634
>>62290695
Definitely below doctors, but also most normies do act super impressed when they hear you’re in law school

62292230
any oldfags in your year?

item.title: cardano, ADA is imploding right now — 59 replies — 13.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
cardano, ADA is imploding right now

Open thread • Replies: 59 • Updated: 2026-05-30 12:37 UTC

OP

cardano, ADA
is imploding right now

Early replies (first 3)

62275698
the whole space is fucking dying. sad that it all ended up this way

62275723
>>62275591
Charles-sama, why?

62276473
>>62275591
Imagine buying cardanus, ever! This shit has less than 500$ stables liquidity onchain.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62280637
>>62277444
But muh Haskell

62280714
>>62280622
>Call me back when stable coins make up less than 99% of all “tokenized real world assets”
DTCC is going to use Chainlink for stablecoins too you know.

62280809
>>62278477
It's a pretty good heuristic

62280833
Save what value you can, swap your ADA for XMR and hodl!

62281020
>>62280595
crypto is banned in india
so in a way, you're like a jeet

62281159
I never trusted Charles so I never bought cardano…dude is an obvious psycho

62281608
ENJOY YOUR CARDANO, EARTHMAN!

62281639
>>62275591
>@TheCardanoTimes

62281653
>>62281159
This, one look at that basedboi faggot should tell you all you need to know

If you still made the decision to put money in this project, its your fault, really

62282581
>>62277151
fucking kek charles is such a fucking narcissist

62282732
>>62277151
Wow that's very bold and stupid. Homeboy thought he could just jump into an industry as complex as healthcare. "Mayo Clinic of the West" lmao

62282767
>>62278448
token not needed

62282801
>>62280714
No they aren't.

62282929
>>62282801

62284091
>>62275698
>>62277437
buy signal

62284100
>>62281020
>crypto is banned in india
just googled it and you're full of shit

62284157
>>62277590
>I remember you could play Super Mario 64 on the Internet Computer Protocol (bing bing wahoo on ICP, jej) but who the fuck cares?
The whole point of that experiment killed ICP the moment it was taken off. What's the point of your web infrastructure if regular web rules still apply to it? Why the fuck is your product worth dick if you're just reinventing the wheel?
>buh muh token costs!!
Irrelevant. If price to run the grid increases because token become more valuable, then whole economic incentive to migrate to your web infrastructure falls apart.

62284265
>>62275591
did they ever get dapps?

62284324
>>62284091
>buy signal

under $20k btc

62284332
>>62284265
i don't think so, who wants to learn haskell

62287225
lol, lmao, etc

62287288
>still worth more than xlm
Tragic

62290899
>>62287225
Just lmfao

62290974
>>62284332
Haskell is arguably one of the worst programming languages ever invented.

62291005
>>62287225
wth is rain
>>62275591
saw this happening when they didn't integrate with Chainlink

62291011
>>62290974
plutus core isn't strictly speaking haskell
it's lambda- calculus
but that's not the reason cardano struggles to have a virtual machine

anyway, cardano can't really "collapse" because its TVL is already very low, there isn't really any money moving, so the security budget of its staking BFT is enough to order everything correctly
but I doubt it will significantly have any real volume either

62291214
https://x.com/IOHK_Charles/status/2060082349963120728

62292080
>>62277437
>t. uninformed retard
Blockchain and DLT actually solve real problems even in regulated markets. You think JPM, Goldman, and the rest of the boomer institutions would dump resources into this just for fun? Use your brain.
It solves a mountain of boring (but expensive) problems that tech-illiterate normies don't even know exist: automated financial workflows, instant settlement, reconciliation, data integrity, and compliance. All baked into a single tamper-proof tech stack.
If you can't see the value in that, just stay quiet and keep posting your 50x memecoin gambles instead of pretending to understand enterprise tech.

62292098
this is all you need to know >>62276491

62292107
>i ready a blog post that told me that big institutions will be buying the token
>i bought the token
>it's been 9 years and nobody is buying the token
>i wasn't scammed though, they will buy the token very soon you guys

item.title: Rescue your dying value — 7 replies — 9.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Rescue your dying value

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-30 16:07 UTC

OP

Swap them for XMR. Purposeless coins are going to 0 by 2028

Early replies (first 3)

62291057
>>62290990
I hope someone posts a cut off graph showing the recent fall in price just so another person can post how quickly we recovered.

62291081
>>62290990
I definitely understand the case for XMR, but ETH now has heavy institutional buying and they're moving stocks/rwa on chain. It's pretty clear if you are watching where elites are pivoting towards in crypto that it's ETH. It's all scripted, guaranteed that Ethereum will tenbag by 2028, all the Jews are paying Congress and they'll pass clarity act by surprise while the public isn't paying attention. BTC is objectively going to get raped by quantum computing, not as an if, but a when.

62291217
>>62291081
No shade to ETH specifically

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62291302
Larry Fink himself said the future they are building is a decentralized banking system for the consumers around the world.

So the future will rely on blockchain and tokens that facilitate this like XRP.

https://youtu.be/5urrOWO0KDI

62291650
>>62290990
Saaar?

62292544
>>62291650
>>62291650
Redeem your memecoins for XMR

62292577
>>62290990
based XMR chad
